fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Louisiana Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to louisiana, speech-language pathologist compact louisiana, louisiana speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 18550 Highland Road, Suite B, Baton Rouge, LA 70809
board.physical_address: 18550 Highland Road, Suite B, Baton Rouge, LA 70809

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Louisiana.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on Praxis (test code 5331, score 162).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

telepractice_text: Louisiana permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. Louisiana is one of the first states actively issuing compact privileges through CompactConnect as of October 2025.
provisional_text: Louisiana issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Louisiana is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact and is actively issuing compact privileges. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Louisiana through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does a Louisiana SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: $125 initial. $75 annual renewal (Louisiana requires annual renewal).

faqs.question: Is Louisiana part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Louisiana is one of the first states actively issuing compact privileges as of October 2025.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Louisiana have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Louisiana participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Louisiana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Louisiana is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact and is actively issuing compact privileges. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Louisiana through a compact privilege.
